candidates.review: RECLASSIFY -- C1 fails: the primary embedding assertion uses cosine_similarity >= 0.999, which is a tolerance-based oracle. Per the contract assertion pattern guidance, tolerance-based assertions are generally contracted. The remaining exact-equality check covers only metadata fields (usage, test label), not generated output. C1 requires the oracle to depend on exact generated text/token/logprob equality, which this test does not.
candidates.comparison: PP+TP config vs TP-only config, cosine similarity >= 0.999 for embeddings + exact equality for metadata
candidates.oracle: cosine_similarity >= 0.999 (tolerance-based) + exact dict equality on non-embedding fields
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: disagree -- the primary assertion is cosine_similarity >= 0.999, a tolerance-based oracle; the contract says tolerance-based assertions are generally contracted, and 0.999 is not unreasonable for cross-config embedding comparison. The exact equality on remaining fields covers only metadata (usage counts, test label), not generated output.
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree -- PP changes computation grouping which can cause numeric drift in embeddings
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree -- Not Strong #4: PP vs TP-only for embeddings has no strong contract

candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree -- PP stage boundaries + multimodal processing changes computation order; PyTorch kernel changes can shift greedy tokens
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree -- Not Strong #4: PP vs TP-only exact generated output equality for multimodal models has no strong contract
candidates.code_snippet: compare_two_settings(model_id, pp_args, tp_args, pp_env, tp_env, method=method)

candidates.review: RECLASSIFY -- Phase 1 missed Strong Contract #4: eager vs cudagraph for the same graph/execution family. The test compares eager_args (cudagraph_args + ["--enforce-eager"]) vs cudagraph_args, both with identical PP_SIZE=2 and identical model/dtype/attention config. The only difference is eager vs cudagraph mode, which is exactly Strong Contract #4.
candidates.comparison: eager+PP vs cudagraph+PP for the same model and config
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree -- exact dict equality on generated text, no tolerance
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: disagree -- cudagraph captures and replays the same kernels as eager mode; CUDA graph replay does not change numeric behavior of captured operations
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: disagree -- Strong Contract #4: eager vs cudagraph for the same graph/execution family. Both configs use PP_SIZE=2 with identical model/dtype; the only toggle is --enforce-eager.
